message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-038 (fix) in tests/test_docs_drift.py
What was done: docs-drift guards made two-sided (exact GOVERNANCE off-box phrase, baseline attribution present, all five ADR-0006 extras + no "four extras").
Regression test(s): test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier, test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest, test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the changed file and the regression tests.

content.content:
1	"""Docs-vs-code drift guards (pass-2 #136, #150, #151, #152, #153, #154).
2	
3	Normative docs make claims a public reader relies on. These guards pin the corrected wording so
4	a regression (re-introducing an over-claim or a stale status) fails CI. They assert on the
5	*specific* stale strings the pass-2 audit found, plus presence of the honest replacement.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
13	
14	
15	def _read(rel: str) -> str:
16	    return (_ROOT / rel).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
17	
18	
19	def test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier():
20	    # #136 + #F-038: the absolute "nothing leaves the machine" over-claim must be qualified — the
21	    # optional high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box. Pin the SPECIFIC high-stakes caveat
22	    # phrase ("not metered, but off-box"), not a loose "off-box" substring: GOVERNANCE has a
23	    # second, unrelated "off-box" occurrence (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so the loose
24	    # check passed even if the high-stakes-tier caveat itself were deleted.
25	    for rel in ("README.md", "docs/CHARTER.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
26	        text = _read(rel)
27	        assert "nothing leaves the machine" not in text, f"{rel}: unconditional over-claim (#136)"
28	        # each file must tie the off-box caveat to the metered/subscription distinction
29	        assert "off-box" in text and "metered" in text, (
30	            f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box / metered caveat missing (#136)"
31	        )
32	    # GOVERNANCE has a SECOND, unrelated "off-box" (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so a
33	    # loose substring passed even if the high-stakes-tier caveat were deleted. Pin its exact
34	    # localized phrase so deleting it fails (#F-038).
35	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
36	    assert "not metered, but off-box" in gov, (
37	        "GOVERNANCE high-stakes off-box caveat removed (#F-038)"
38	    )
39	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0003-local-first-data-sovereign.md")
40	    assert "off-box" in adr and "the same as" in adr  # billing vs residency separated
41	
42	
43	def test_charter_status_is_not_stale():
44	    # #150: the status line must not still call the seams "stubs being wired".
45	    charter = _read("docs/CHARTER.md")
46	    assert "stubs being wired" not in charter
47	    assert "implemented and composed" in charter or "in place and hardened" in charter
48	
49	
50	def test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest():
51	    # #151 + #F-038: the engine ships primitives + a determinism harness, NOT embedding/clustering/
52	    # graph builders — those are adapter-supplied. Two-sided: assert the POSITIVE attribution
53	    # (the choice-bearing builders come from the adapter's BaselineBuilder, not the engine), so a
54	    # regression re-introducing an "engine ships builders" claim — which leaves "BaselineBuilder"
55	    # in the text and so passed the old single-substring check — now fails.
56	    arch = _read("docs/ARCHITECTURE.md")
57	    init = _read("src/assay_engine/__init__.py")
58	    for text in (arch, init):
59	        assert "BaselineBuilder" in text
60	    assert "not shipped by the engine" in arch, "ARCHITECTURE missing the explicit non-ship caveat"
61	    assert "supplied per study by" in init, "__init__ missing the adapter-attribution caveat"
62	
63	
64	def test_preregistration_timestamp_wording_matches_shipped_default():
65	    # #152: body text must not present RFC-3161 as the in-force mechanism; HMAC is the default.
66	    for rel in ("docs/METHODOLOGY.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
67	        text = _read(rel)
68	        assert "HMAC" in text, f"{rel}: shipped HMAC default not stated (#152)"
69	        assert "pluggable" in text, f"{rel}: RFC-3161 must be described as pluggable (#152)"
70	    # Guard the SPECIFIC stale over-claims, so re-introducing either one fails CI even though the
71	    # word "HMAC" appears elsewhere in the file (file-level substring checks alone don't catch a
72	    # localized regression — the pass-2 confirmation flagged this on GOVERNANCE §2 step 3).
73	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
74	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority" not in gov, (
75	        "GOVERNANCE §2 step 3 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
76	    )
77	    meth = _read("docs/METHODOLOGY.md")
78	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped before confirmation" not in meth, (
79	        "METHODOLOGY §7 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
80	    )
81	
82	
83	def test_readme_install_notes_not_on_pypi():
84	    # #153: the quickstart must not present `pip install assay-engine` as a working path without
85	    # the not-yet-on-PyPI caveat + a from-source path.
86	    readme = _read("README.md")
87	    assert "Not yet on PyPI" in readme
88	    assert "pip install -e" in readme  # the working from-source path
89	
90	
91	def test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra():
92	    # #154 + #F-038: ADR-0006's extras enumeration must include `baseline` AND must not understate
93	    # the count. Two-sided: assert every current extra name is present (so dropping any one fails)
94	    # and that no stale "four extras" undercount survives alongside.
95	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md")
96	    for extra in (
97	        "`reasoning`",
98	        "`observability`",
99	        "`persistence`",
100	        "`orchestration`",
101	        "`baseline`",
102	    ):
103	        assert extra in adr, f"ADR-0006 extras enumeration missing {extra} (#F-038)"
104	    assert "four extras" not in adr, "ADR-0006 understates the extras count (stale 'four extras')"
105

content.text: Now let me verify the docs files actually contain the asserted phrases, so the tests truly pass against current content and discriminate.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && echo "=== GOVERNANCE: not metered, but off-box ===" && grep -n "not metered, but off-box" docs/GOVERNANCE.md; echo "=== GOVERNANCE off-box occurrences ===" && grep -nc "off-box" docs/GOVERNANCE.md; echo "=== ARCHITECTURE not shipped by the engine ===" && grep -n "not shipped by the engine" docs/ARCHITECTURE.md; echo "=== __init__ supplied per study by ===" && grep -n "supplied per study by" src/assay_engine/__init__.py; echo "=== __init__ BaselineBuilder ===" && grep -n "BaselineBuilder" src/assay_engine/__init__.py; echo "=== ARCH BaselineBuilder ===" && grep -n "BaselineBuilder" docs/ARCHITECTURE.md

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && echo "=== ADR-0006 extras ===" && grep -nE '`reasoning`|`observability`|`persistence`|`orchestration`|`baseline`' docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md; echo "=== ADR-0006 four extras ===" && grep -n "four extras" docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md; echo "exit-fourextras=$?"

content.text: All three pass. Now let me verify discrimination: would each fail if the fix were reverted? The key claim is the tests are now "two-sided." Let me check the git history to see what the old single-sided assertions looked like, confirming the new assertions genuinely add discrimination.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -3 -- tests/test_docs_drift.py && echo "=== diff of F-038 changes ===" && git log -p -1 -S "not metered, but off-box" -- tests/test_docs_drift.py 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[+-]" | grep -iE "off-box|not shipped|supplied per|baseline|four extras|F-038" | head -40

content.content:
ba82429 fix(hardening-p3): batch 6 — two-sided docs-drift guards (F-038)
1e3dce5 harden/pass-2: 33 production-readiness fixes (#124-#156) (#157)
=== diff of F-038 changes ===
-    # high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box.
+    # #136 + #F-038: the absolute "nothing leaves the machine" over-claim must be qualified — the
+    # optional high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box. Pin the SPECIFIC high-stakes caveat
+    # phrase ("not metered, but off-box"), not a loose "off-box" substring: GOVERNANCE has a
+    # second, unrelated "off-box" occurrence (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so the loose
-        assert "off-box" in text, f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box caveat missing (#136)"
+        # each file must tie the off-box caveat to the metered/subscription distinction
+        assert "off-box" in text and "metered" in text, (
+            f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box / metered caveat missing (#136)"
+    # GOVERNANCE has a SECOND, unrelated "off-box" (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so a
+    # localized phrase so deleting it fails (#F-038).
+    assert "not metered, but off-box" in gov, (
+        "GOVERNANCE high-stakes off-box caveat removed (#F-038)"
+    # #151 + #F-038: the engine ships primitives + a determinism harness, NOT embedding/clustering/
+    # (the choice-bearing builders come from the adapter's BaselineBuilder, not the engine), so a
+    # regression re-introducing an "engine ships builders" claim — which leaves "BaselineBuilder"
-        # the corrected text names the adapter's BaselineBuilder as the source of choice-bearing
+    assert "not shipped by the engine" in arch, "ARCHITECTURE missing the explicit non-ship caveat"
+    assert "supplied per study by" in init, "__init__ missing the adapter-attribution caveat"
-    # #154: ADR-0006's extras enumeration must include `baseline` (pyproject has five extras).
+    # #154 + #F-038: ADR-0006's extras enumeration must include `baseline` AND must not understate
+    # and that no stale "four extras" undercount survives alongside.
-    assert "`baseline`" in adr
+        "`baseline`",
+        assert extra in adr, f"ADR-0006 extras enumeration missing {extra} (#F-038)"
+    assert "four extras" not in adr, "ADR-0006 understates the extras count (stale 'four extras')"

content.text: The diff confirms the genuine two-sided strengthening. Let me verify each test discriminates by simulating reverting the doc fix (deleting the localized phrase) and confirming the test would fail. I'll do this with an in-memory check rather than mutating files.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
# Discrimination check: if the off-box high-stakes caveat were deleted but the
# OTHER off-box (SaaS disqualification) remained, does the new assertion catch it?
gov = open("docs/GOVERNANCE.md").read()
# simulate deleting the high-stakes caveat line only
reverted = gov.replace("(not metered, but off-box)", "")
print("loose 'off-box' still present after revert:", "off-box" in reverted)  # True -> old check missed it
print("exact 'not metered, but off-box' present after revert:", "not metered, but off-box" in reverted)  # False -> new check catches

arch = open("docs/ARCHITECTURE.md").read()
rev_arch = arch.replace("not shipped by the engine", "shipped by the engine")
print("BaselineBuilder still present after revert:", "BaselineBuilder" in rev_arch)  # old check missed
print("'not shipped by the engine' present after revert:", "not shipped by the engine" in rev_arch)  # new catches

adr = open("docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md").read()
rev_adr = adr.replace("`baseline`", "")
print("baseline present after revert:", "`baseline`" in rev_adr)  # new catches absence
EOF

content.content:
loose 'off-box' still present after revert: True
exact 'not metered, but off-box' present after revert: False
BaselineBuilder still present after revert: True
'not shipped by the engine' present after revert: False
baseline present after revert: False

content.text:
Discrimination is confirmed empirically. For each of the three regression tests, reverting the doc fix while leaving the decoy/old-substring intact would now fail the new assertion (whereas the old loose substring check passed). 

Summary of findings:
- The fix correctly makes all three docs-drift guards two-sided. Each pins a specific, localized phrase rather than a loose substring that a decoy occurrence (the second "off-box", the surviving "BaselineBuilder", the still-present "baseline" token) could satisfy after a regression.
- The underlying docs (`docs/GOVERNANCE.md:132`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:22`, `src/assay_engine/__init__.py:16-17`, `docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md:21-22`) actually contain the asserted phrases, so the tests pass on current content.
- Discrimination verified: simulated revert of each doc fix (leaving the decoy intact) breaks the new assertion in every case, while the old loose check would have passed.
- The "four extras" negative guard and the full five-extra enumeration are both present and correct. ADR-0006 has no "four extras" undercount.
- All three named tests pass.

Idiomatic, no regression, complete with discriminating tests.

input.reasoning:
The fix makes three docs-drift guards genuinely two-sided, and I verified each both passes on current content and discriminates against a reverted fix.

1. test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier: Now pins the exact localized phrase "not metered, but off-box" (docs/GOVERNANCE.md:132) plus per-file "off-box"+"metered". GOVERNANCE has a SECOND, unrelated "off-box" (the SaaS-observability disqualification, line 129), so the old loose `"off-box" in text` check passed even if the high-stakes caveat were deleted. Simulated revert confirms: loose "off-box" survives (old check missed it) but "not metered, but off-box" disappears (new check fails). Discriminates.

2. test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest: Adds positive attribution asserts — "not shipped by the engine" (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:22) and "supplied per study by" (src/assay_engine/__init__.py:16). The old check only required "BaselineBuilder" present, which would survive an "engine ships builders" regression. Simulated revert confirms BaselineBuilder survives but the new phrase fails. Discriminates.

3. test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra: Now asserts all five extra names (reasoning/observability/persistence/orchestration/baseline) individually AND `"four extras" not in adr`. All five present at ADR-0006 lines 21-22; "four extras" absent. Dropping any extra or reintroducing the undercount now fails. Discriminates.

All three named tests pass (.venv pytest, 3 passed). Empirical revert simulation confirms each new assertion breaks when the doc fix is reverted while the decoy substring remains. Idiomatic, no regression, complete.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. The F-038 fix is both appropriate and complete — the three docs-drift guards are genuinely two-sided, pass on current content, and each discriminates against a reverted fix (verified via simulated revert where the decoy substring survives but the new localized assertion fails).
